old_claim.note: Perspective in Art provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because the node is now scoped to Leonardo-era tonal transitions and light-dark modelling; perspective is adjacent Renaissance visual practice but not sourced as a prerequisite for sfumato or chiaroscuro.

source_shape.source_locator: The National Gallery explains The Virgin of the Rocks through chiaroscuro defining three-dimensional figures and sfumato's smoky transitions between light and shadow, without making linear perspective a prerequisite.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source grounds the technique in tonal modelling and smoky light-shadow transitions rather than perspectival construction.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Perspective may be relevant to broader Renaissance pictorial space, but this scoped node is about light-dark modelling and edge transitions, so perspective is not a direct edge.

ontology_before: Sfumato & Chiaroscuro depended directly on perspective_in_art, implying linear perspective enabled the tonal and edge-transition techniques.
ontology_after: Leonardo Sfumato & Chiaroscuro depends directly on oil painting as the material technique evidenced for light-dark and sfumato effects; perspective is excluded as adjacent context.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: sfumato_chiaroscuro_techniques no longer directly depends on perspective_in_art.
would_reject_if: The node is later rescoped to a broader Leonardo pictorial-system node that includes perspectival projection.
A stronger source explicitly states that Renaissance linear perspective was a direct prerequisite for sfumato or chiaroscuro technique.
A separate perspective-lighting-composition node is introduced and should carry the perspective dependency.
short_reason: The source-backed mechanism is tonal light-shadow modelling, not linear perspective.
